macro_events.event_summary: Asian markets brace for a mixed open as US Treasury yields spike and Middle East tensions with Iran escalate.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end Treasury yields raise discount rates and pressure duration-sensitive assets
Iran geopolitical risk premium on crude oil and defense spending
Risk-off rotation toward energy and defense, away from rate-sensitive growth

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with high leverage to crude prices; Iran tension typically adds a $3-8 geopolitical risk premium to Brent which flows directly to upstream earnings.
Pure-play US shale producer with high oil-price beta (no refining offset); benefits most from a geopolitical crude premium.
Largest US defense prime; Iran escalation supports missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) demand and reinforces FY budget tailwinds.
Raytheon missile/air defense exposure (Patriot, SM-3, Stinger) is direct beneficiary of Middle East restocking cycles.
Strategic deterrence and ISR platforms benefit from elevated Middle East tension and higher defense allocations.
Oilfield services activity ramps with sustained higher crude prices; international rig count most sensitive.

losers.rationale: Airlines are squeezed by both higher jet fuel (Iran/crude) and weaker risk sentiment; jet fuel is ~25-30% of opex.
Southwest has lower fuel hedging than peers historically and is highly exposed to a crude spike with limited pricing power.
Realty Income — bond-proxy REIT with high duration sensitivity; rising yields compress relative dividend yield appeal.

headlines: Asia markets poised for mixed open amid spike in Treasury yields, Iran tensions
